evals.prompt: Install GitHub spec-kit in this repo and make Codex use its skills instead of slash prompt files.
evals.expected_output: Uses the official Specify CLI from github/spec-kit, initializes with --integration codex --integration-options="--skills", and verifies .agents/skills/speckit-* exists.

evals.prompt: The docs say to run /speckit.plan, but I am in Codex. What should I invoke?
evals.expected_output: Explains the Codex equivalent $speckit-plan and points to the slash-command mapping.

evals.prompt: I have a feature idea but no requirements yet. Should I run tasks or implement?
evals.expected_output: Chooses specify first, optionally clarify, then plan and tasks before implement.

evals.prompt: Show me how to switch an initialized Spec Kit project from Copilot to Codex skills.
evals.expected_output: Uses specify integration switch codex with integration options, warns that only one integration is active, and preserves local modifications.

evals.prompt: Write a unit test for this React component.
evals.expected_output: Does not route to Spec Kit unless the user asks for spec-driven feature artifacts or Spec Kit commands.

evals.prompt: Create a general Agent Skill for editing spreadsheets.
evals.expected_output: Uses general skill-authoring guidance, not Spec Kit, unless the skill is about Spec Kit or SDD workflows.
